title: OIG: Winn Correctional Center Staff Used Banned Chokehold; Three of Five Use-of-Force Cases Violated Standards
excerpt: A DHS OIG unannounced inspection of the Winn Correctional Center in Louisiana found that staff used a prohibited chokehold on a detainee and violated use-of-force standards in three of five reviewed incidents. The report also found sanitation deficiencies and inadequate legal access at the facility, which held 1,576 detainees at maximum capacity.
content:
On June 2, 2026, the DHS Office of Inspector General published OIG-26-08, the results of an unannounced inspection of the Winn Correctional Center in Winnfield, Louisiana — one of the largest ICE detention centers in the country.

  • Inspectors reviewed five use-of-force incidents and found that facility staff used prohibited techniques or failed to follow standards in three of them.
  • In one case, an officer used a chokehold on a detainee during a physical altercation — a technique explicitly prohibited under ICE detention standards.
  • In another case, staff placed mechanical restraints and a suicide smock on a detainee who refused orders, but failed to document the required medical review on camera.
  • At the time of inspection, the facility held 1,576 male detainees — its contractual maximum capacity.
  • Inspectors also found sanitation problems including holes in the ceiling, water dripping onto floors, and leaking kitchen vents, as well as inadequate legal access for detainees.

The OIG issued nine recommendations; ICE concurred with all nine. Four were resolved and closed; five remained open pending proof of corrective action. Rep. Pramila Jayapal called the report "damning" and said it documented "serious violations of standards for food safety, medical care, and use of force."

DHS OIG said ICE detention standards specifically prohibit chokeholds during use-of-force incidents.
mediaPlaceholder: Cover page of OIG-26-08 inspection report for Winn Correctional Center documenting use-of-force violations
